source: AAMC — What's on the MCAT Exam? Content Outline

chemPhys.name: Chemical & Physical Foundations of Biological Systems

bioBiochem.name: Biological & Biochemical Foundations of Living Systems

psychSoc.name: Psychological, Social & Biological Foundations of Behavior

scienceSkills.title: Knowledge of scientific concepts and principles
scienceSkills.summary: Recognize, recall, define, and connect core science concepts.

scienceSkills.title: Scientific reasoning and problem-solving
scienceSkills.summary: Use theories, models, formulas, and mechanisms to solve unfamiliar problems.

scienceSkills.title: Design and execution of research
scienceSkills.summary: Evaluate hypotheses, variables, controls, measurement, validity, reliability, and ethics.

scienceSkills.title: Data-based and statistical reasoning
scienceSkills.summary: Read tables, graphs, figures, data patterns, statistics, and experimental results.

carsSkills.title: Foundations of comprehension
carsSkills.summary: Understand main ideas, details, structure, and rhetorical purpose.

carsSkills.title: Reasoning within the text
carsSkills.summary: Infer meaning, evaluate arguments, and follow the author's logic.

carsSkills.title: Reasoning beyond the text
carsSkills.summary: Apply ideas to new contexts and judge implications outside the passage.

concepts.title: Biomolecules have unique properties that determine how they contribute to living systems.
concepts.summary: Amino acids, proteins, enzymes, DNA/RNA, gene expression, and bioenergetics.

categories.title: Structure and function of proteins and their constituent amino acids
Transmission of genetic information from the gene to the protein
Transmission of heritable information from generation to generation
Principles of bioenergetics and fuel molecule metabolism

concepts.title: Highly organized assemblies of molecules, cells, and organs interact to carry out life processes.
concepts.summary: Cells, membranes, organ systems, and biological organization.

categories.title: Assemblies of molecules, cells, and groups of cells within organisms
The structure, growth, physiology, and genetics of prokaryotes and viruses
Processes of cell division, differentiation, and specialization

concepts.title: Complex systems of tissues and organs sense environments and maintain internal stability.
concepts.summary: Nervous, endocrine, sensory, muscular, skeletal, immune, digestive, excretory, reproductive, respiratory, and circulatory systems.

categories.title: Structure and functions of nervous and endocrine systems
Structure and integrative functions of main organ systems

concepts.title: Physical principles explain interactions among fluids, gases, charges, and light in living systems.
concepts.summary: Fluids, gases, electrochemistry, circuits, light, optics, and sound.

categories.title: Translational motion, forces, work, energy, and equilibrium
Importance of fluids for circulation, gas movement, and gas exchange
How light and sound interact with matter
Atoms, nuclear decay, electronic structure, and atomic chemical behavior

concepts.title: Chemical principles govern the behavior of molecules in biological systems.
concepts.summary: Bonding, solutions, thermodynamics, kinetics, equilibrium, acids/bases, separations, and organic reactivity.

categories.title: Unique nature of water and its solutions
Structure, function, and reactivity of biologically relevant molecules
Principles of chemical thermodynamics and kinetics

concepts.title: Biological, psychological, and sociocultural factors influence perception and behavior.
concepts.summary: Sensing, perception, consciousness, cognition, emotion, stress, and behavior.

concepts.title: Behavior is shaped by learning, memory, identity, attitudes, and social processes.
concepts.summary: Learning, memory, identity, social thinking, attitudes, and behavior change.

concepts.title: Psychological, sociocultural, and biological factors influence health and illness.
concepts.summary: Health behavior, mental health, social determinants, and healthcare access.

concepts.title: Social structures and demographics shape human behavior and population health.
concepts.summary: Culture, society, social institutions, demographics, and social stratification.

concepts.title: Social stratification and access to resources influence well-being.
concepts.summary: Inequality, social class, privilege, mobility, and institutional effects on health.

concepts.title: CARS measures comprehension, analysis, and reasoning from humanities and social science passages.
concepts.summary: Passage-bound reasoning, author logic, claims, evidence, implications, and careful answer-choice evaluation.
